name: Chicago Deep-Dish Pizza
recipeInstructions:
Dough: Dissolve yeast in the 1/2 cup warm water. Add sugar and stir well.
Set aside. In a large mixing bowl, combine 3 1/2 cups flour, salt, and cornmeal.
Make a well in the center of the flour. Add yeast mixture, vegetable oil,
and 1/2 cup water. Stir thoroughly until the dough cleans the sides
of the bowl and forms a rough mass.
Turn dough out onto a well-floured work surface. Knead and pound the dough
(dust with flour if it sticks to your hands) for 5-6 minutes, until smooth
and soft. Dust the dough and a large mixing bowl lightly with flour.
Place dough in the bowl, cover with plastic wrap and a kitchen towel,
and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 1/2 hours.
After the dough has doubled, turn it out of the bowl and knead for
about 2 minutes. Oil the bottom and sides of the pizza pan. Spread the
dough in the pan with your fingers and palm (it will spread more easily
if you let it sit in the pan for about 10 min.). Work the dough until
it covers the bottom of the pan.
Pull the edges of the dough up to form a lip or pronounced border around
the pan. Preheat oven to 475°F. Prick the dough bottom at 1/2-inch intervals.
Let rise for 30 minutes. Parbake for exactly 4 minutes. Brush crust with
olive oil or butter.
Topping: In a bowl, combine tomatoes, basil, oregano, and salt.
Assembly: Lay slices of mozzarella cheese evenly over the crust.
Spoon tomatoes over the cheese. Sprinkle grated Parmesan cheese over the
tomatoes. Add Italian sausage. Drizzle about 1 tablespoon olive oil on top.
Bake pizza in a preheated oven at 500°F on the bottom rack for 5 minutes.
Move the pizza to an oven rack two levels above the lower rack and bake
an additional 15 minutes, until crust is lightly browned and sausage is cooked through.
